label: Graph-of-Thought & non-linear deliberation

node.label: Graph-of-Thought & non-linear deliberation
node.definition: Graph-of-Thought methods represent reasoning states as a graph so branches can merge, share evidence, revisit conclusions, or combine partial solutions.
node.why_it_matters: They capture synthesis and dependency patterns that a strict chain or tree cannot represent efficiently.
limitations: Graph construction and credit assignment are difficult, and unconstrained merging can compound inconsistent assumptions.

dependencies.rationale: Graph-of-Thought & non-linear deliberation explores alternatives using the state, scoring, or search process in Tree-of-Thought & branch-and-evaluate reasoning.
Graph-of-Thought & non-linear deliberation explores alternatives using the state, scoring, or search process in Graph theory.
In this atlas graph, Graph-of-Thought & non-linear deliberation has a `searches_with` dependency on Multi-agent coordination, debate & markets with `common` strength.
Graph-of-Thought & non-linear deliberation explores alternatives using the state, scoring, or search process in Reflection, critique, verifier & repair loops.
